Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Exhaled gas by Calculated --at end expiration

en.name: End tidal carbon dioxide (PetCO2)
en.shortDescription: End-tidal CO2 as a partial pressure (PetCO2), in mmHg/kPa. The % (fraction) form is a separate set.
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context End-tidal carbon dioxide (EtCO2) is the carbon dioxide in the gas a patient exhales at the very end of a breath, measured continuously by a capnograph on the breathing circuit. It is the standard non-invasive surrogate for arterial CO2 and is used to confirm tube placement, monitor ventilation, and detect changes in cardiac output. This set captures EtCO2 expressed as a **partial pressure (PetCO2)**, reported in **mmHg** (or kPa). In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, recorded in intensive care, anaesthesia, and emergency settings for ventilated patients. (EtCO2 expressed as a volume fraction in % is a separate, non-inter-convertible measurement — see Mapping Notes.) ## Included Concepts - *4353938 / 250784008 — End tidal carbon dioxide concentration (SNOMED)*: the generic end-tidal CO2 concept; the default, recommended in mmHg (the usual ICU representation). - *3035357 / 19891-1 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Exhaled gas --at end expiration (LOINC)*: measured PetCO2. - *3035115 / 19890-3 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Exhaled gas by Calculated --at end expiration (LOINC)*: calculated PetCO2. **Note on units**: reported in **`8876 / mm[Hg] — millimeter mercury column`** (UCUM), the recommended unit, with a registered mmHg↔kPa conversion (1 mmHg = 0.133 kPa, 1 kPa = 7.50 mmHg). ## Mapping Notes - Map a PetCO2 value (mmHg, or kPa converted with ×7.50) to one of these concepts. Use the generic *4353938 / 250784008* when the source does not specify measured vs calculated. - **Partial pressure vs fraction are not inter-convertible**: EtCO2 expressed as a volume fraction (%) belongs to the separate **End tidal carbon dioxide fraction** concept set. The two are not related by a fixed factor — PetCO2 (mmHg) is the fraction multiplied by the barometric pressure [1] — so they are kept as separate, unit-homogeneous sets. - **Not arterial CO2**: arterial CO2 partial pressure (PaCO2, from a blood gas) is a different measurement in its own concept set. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 19890-3 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Exhaled gas by Calculated --at end expiration. Available: https://loinc.org/19890-3/

fr.name: CO2 télé-expiratoire (PetCO2)
fr.shortDescription: CO2 télé-expiratoire en pression partielle (PetCO2), en mmHg/kPa. La forme en % (fraction) est un set distinct.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Split EtCO2 by unit family (units must be inter-convertible within a set). This set now holds only the partial-pressure (PetCO2, mmHg/kPa) concepts + the generic SNOMED concept; the volume-fraction (%) concepts moved to the new End tidal carbon dioxide fraction set (361). Renamed to "End tidal carbon dioxide (PetCO2)".
